is n8n cheaper than zapier — Complete Guide [2026]

AI AI Tools Idea · May 22, 2026 · Updated May 22, 2026
— min read
Affiliate disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our links — at no extra cost to you. Learn more →
🚀 AI Platform

As a small business owner, you’re likely struggling to find the right automation tool to streamline your workflow and increase productivity, with one of the main questions on your mind being: is n8n cheaper than Zapier. With so many options available, it can be overwhelming to choose the best tool for your business. In this article, we’ll compare n8n and Zapier, two popular automation tools, to help you make an informed decision.

Zapier is a well-established player in the automation market, with a wide range of features and integrations. Try Zapier free to see if it’s the right fit for your business. But is it the most cost-effective option? Let’s take a closer look at the pricing plans for both n8n and Zapier.

Here’s a pricing table comparing the different plans offered by n8n and Zapier:

Plann8nZapier
FreeUnlimited workflows, 100 tasks/month100 tasks/month, 5 zaps
Starter$25/month, 1,000 tasks/month$19.99/month, 100 tasks/month, 20 zaps
Pro$49/month, 10,000 tasks/month$49/month, 1,000 tasks/month, 100 zaps
Team$99/month, 50,000 tasks/month$299/month, 5,000 tasks/month, 500 zaps
CompanyCustom pricing, 100,000+ tasks/monthCustom pricing, 10,000+ tasks/month

As you can see, n8n offers a more generous free plan, with unlimited workflows and 100 tasks/month. However, Zapier’s paid plans are more affordable, especially for small businesses with limited automation needs.

Now, let’s weigh the pros and cons of using Zapier for your small business.

Pros:

  • Easy to use: Zapier has a user-friendly interface that makes it easy to create and manage automations, even for non-technical users.
  • Wide range of integrations: Zapier integrates with over 1,000 apps, including popular tools like Google Drive, Trello, and Slack.
  • Reliable customer support: Zapier offers excellent customer support, with a comprehensive knowledge base and responsive support team.
  • Scalable: Zapier’s plans are designed to grow with your business, with custom pricing available for large enterprises.
  • Pro tip: Use Zapier’s built-in filtering and formatting tools to customize your automations and reduce errors.

Cons:

  • Limited workflow complexity: Zapier’s workflow editor can be limiting for complex automations, making it difficult to create multi-step workflows.
  • Dependence on third-party apps: Zapier relies on third-party apps to function, which can lead to integration issues and downtime.
  • Cost: While Zapier’s paid plans are affordable, the cost can add up quickly, especially for large businesses with multiple users.
  • Pro tip: Use Zapier’s “Paths” feature to create conditional logic and split your workflows based on specific conditions.
  • Limited analytics: Zapier’s analytics and reporting features are limited, making it difficult to track the performance of your automations.

Who should avoid this: If you’re a small business with very basic automation needs, you may find Zapier’s pricing plans too expensive. Additionally, if you’re looking for a high degree of customization and control over your automations, you may find Zapier’s workflow editor limiting. In these cases, n8n or other automation tools may be a better fit.

Pro tip: Before committing to a paid plan, Try Zapier free to test its features and integrations. This will give you a better understanding of whether Zapier is the right tool for your business.

In terms of cost, Zapier is generally more affordable than n8n, especially for small businesses with limited automation needs. However, n8n’s free plan is more generous, and its custom pricing plans can be more cost-effective for large enterprises.

To get the most out of Zapier, it’s essential to understand its limitations and potential drawbacks. By being aware of these, you can plan your automations accordingly and avoid common pitfalls. Try Zapier free to see if it’s the right fit for your business.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the main difference between n8n and Zapier?

The main difference between n8n and Zapier is their approach to automation. n8n is an open-source tool that offers more customization and control over automations, while Zapier is a cloud-based tool that focuses on ease of use and simplicity. n8n is also more geared towards developers, while Zapier is designed for non-technical users.

Can I use Zapier for free?

Yes, Zapier offers a free plan that includes 100 tasks/month and 5 zaps. This plan is suitable for small businesses with limited automation needs. However, if you need more tasks or zaps, you’ll need to upgrade to a paid plan.

How do I integrate Zapier with other apps?

Zapier integrates with over 1,000 apps, including popular tools like Google Drive, Trello, and Slack. To integrate Zapier with other apps, simply search for the app in Zapier’s app directory and follow the instructions to connect your account. You can then create automations using Zapier’s workflow editor.

What kind of support does Zapier offer?

Zapier offers excellent customer support, with a comprehensive knowledge base and responsive support team. You can contact Zapier’s support team via email or live chat, and they’ll be happy to help you with any questions or issues you have. Additionally, Zapier has a large community of users and developers who contribute to its forums and documentation.
